# Transcrição: 08. How to Select Primary Themes

- **Fonte:** 08. How to Select Primary Themes
- **Duração:** 01:48
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:48]

This is getting to the exciting point that we all love in this process. We're only a few minutes away from getting to the initial draft of your why statement. What you need to do before that is to review your list of contributions and your list of impacts and select the ones that really stand out. You need to pick one contribution and one impact. In a previous video, we asked you to circle any themes that jumped out at you. Start with those. The words might need a little tweaking, but the idea should feel right. Start by selecting your impact. Your impact is the desired end state you strive for. Which impact really hits home? Which describes the difference you want to make in the world or the world in which you want to live? When I worked with my partner, we realized the things I did when at my best were all aimed at something specific. I believe that everyone can achieve extraordinary things regardless of who they are or their specific circumstances. Even though I knew the language needed some adjustment, I selected achieve extraordinary things as my impact. Next, you'll need to select a primary contribution. When you read through your contributions, you should nod and think to yourself, yeah, I do all these things when I'm at my best. However, one likely feels more important than the rest. For Peter, this was to enable others. If you love more than one of your contribution themes, that's great. We'll use those in part three when it's time to define your hows. Hows are the everyday actions you take to bring your life to life? More about that later. If you're having trouble identifying your primary contribution and impact, you might like to watch this optional video for further guidance.
